description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
An unoccupied Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was traveling south on Bryant Street, slowing and preparing to make a right turn onto 
Division Street in San Francisco. At that time, an approaching e-scooterist was also traveling south on Bryant Street in the curb lane offset 
from the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle braked to a stop as the e-scooterist was beginning to enter the intersection. The e-scooterist then 
made contact with the right rear side of the stopped Zoox vehicle, and the e-scooterist fell to the ground next to the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox 
vehicle then started to move and completed the right turn onto Treat Avenue, coming to a stop a short distance down Treat Avenue. The 
e-scooterist then walked themself and the e-scooter over to the sidewalk, where they were met by a member of the Zoox operations team, 
and they reported “[a] chipped tooth and jaw pain.” The e-scooterist declined medical attention and rode their e-scooter from the scene after 
exchanging information. There was no damage to the Zoox vehicle and the police were not called.
Print
Clear Form
